mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns follow payroll growth in fewer than 55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.5 percentage point rise in the unemployment rate over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and declines in industrial production identify a monetary tightening into a slowing real economy, which tightens financial conditions and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over main effects.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, a 3-month acceleration in consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to hike faster than discounted, raising real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this mechanism is inactive outside tightening regimes.
failure_condition: In tightening regimes, positive 20-day returns follow the signal in more than 45% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals resilient consumer spending that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with positive retail sales growth over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in industrial production growth indicates expanding manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in housing starts signal weakening construction activity and downstream demand for durables, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered episodes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month increase in the federal funds rate greater than 0.25 percentage points signals an abrupt monetary tightening that raises short-term discount rates and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns follow the trigger in fewer than 60% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.
